commit_message: feat(adaptive-crawler): add shouldPropagateError callback to prevent browser fallback (#3635)
files_analyzed: packages/playwright-crawler/src/internals/adaptive-playwright-crawler.ts (first 400 lines including class signature, options interface, _runRequestHandler logic)
packages/core/src/autoscaling/autoscaled_pool.ts (first 250 lines: options interface + AutoscaledPool class header)
packages/core/src/autoscaling/ — file listing (snapshotter.ts, system_status.ts, load signals)
packages/core/src/ — file listing (configuration, crawlers, enqueue_links, errors, events, http_clients, proxy_configuration, request, router, session_pool, storages)
packages/utils/src/internals/ — file listing (gotScraping, social, robots, sitemap, extract-urls)
files_intentionally_not_analyzed: packages/browser-pool/ — fingerprint logic is in apify/fingerprint-suite (separate repo)
packages/cheerio/ — substituted by Adaptive HTTP mode
packages/jsdom/, packages/linkedom/ — used internally by Adaptive
packages/playwright-crawler/src/internals/utils/rendering-type-prediction.ts — algorithm internals not needed (use as black box)
extraction_method: gh api (REST GitHub) — git clone blocked in sandbox
key_findings: AdaptivePlaywrightCrawler is THE killer feature — HTTP-first with browser fallback yields 3-4x speedup expected on portal listing pages (mostly SSR).
shouldPropagateError callback (added in commit d3a29d96, 2026-05-11) is critical: lets us bypass browser fallback for 403/429/Cloudflare — exactly what we need for portal anti-bot.
AutoscaledPool's default maxConcurrency=200 is WAY too aggressive for BR portals — must override to 20.
taskTimeoutSecs default = 0 (Infinity) is dangerous — must always set explicit (60s).
parseWithCheerio() in context is the abstraction that makes user handler portable between HTTP-only and browser modes.
Statistics subclass tracks httpOnlyRequestHandlerRuns, browserRequestHandlerRuns, renderingTypeMispredictions — directly observable as SLOs.
transferability_to_epic7: DIRECT — Crawlee is THE framework decision from Wave 2
epic7_actions_recommended: Implement packages/scrapers/lib/portal-crawler.ts wrapper enforcing safe defaults
Implement Telemetry layer pushing to Supabase tables crawl_runs / crawl_requests / crawl_failures
Use AdaptivePlaywrightCrawler with renderingTypeDetectionRatio=0.1, shouldPropagateError for 403/429/CF
Use Apify Platform Storage in Wave A (default), evaluate Supabase storage adapter for Wave B
Pin crawlee version exact in package.json (AdaptivePlaywrightCrawler is @experimental)
